final_response_text:
The follow-up information suggests the probability of a good outcome (presumably returning 2x+ or some meaningful positive return) should be revised downward from my initial 15% estimate.

Key considerations:
- Base rate of 2x+ return is only ~5%
- The "friends and family" discount may not be meaningful, so no edge over base rate
- The person offering has misaligned incentives, which if anything suggests adverse selection (they may be pushing harder when the deal is weaker)

These factors all point in the same direction — downward. The adverse selection and lack of real discount might even push below the 5% base rate. However, there could be some information value in being offered the deal (the company exists, has some momentum), so I won't go far below the base rate.

I'll revise to approximately 5%, reflecting roughly the base rate with no meaningful edge from the discount and slight negative signal from incentive misalignment.
